mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or positive return frequency drops below 55% during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.5%, labor market slack signals weakening consumer demand and rising recession probability, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE crosses 5.5%, or the signal loses statistical significance (p>0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales combined with accelerating inflation indicates demand-pull price pressures that force monetary tightening expectations, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when RSAFS growth exceeds 3% and CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 2% simultaneously, or interaction term coefficient is insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production declines during a post-shock recovery phase signal supply chain bottlenecks and margin compression, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines yield positive 5-day returns in >65% of cases during post-shock recovery periods, or the mechanism shows no predictive power outside the specified regime.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and corporate revenue growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero correlation with 60-day forward returns over a 5-year rolling window, or positive return frequency falls below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 1.8 million units signals a sharp contraction in construction activity and consumer confidence, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 returns remain positive in >60% of months where HOUST falls below 1800.0, or the threshold effect vanishes in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: High federal funds rates combined with slowing GDP growth indicate a restrictive monetary policy environment that suppresses corporate earnings, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The interaction between FEDFUNDS and GDPC1 fails to predict negative returns when rates exceed 4.0% and GDP growth falls below 2.0%, or the joint effect is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth outpacing inflation during a supply-led expansion phase signals robust corporate margins and inventory buildup, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in <50% of instances where INDPRO growth exceeds CPIAUCSL growth during supply-led expansions, or the signal reverses direction in out-of-sample tests.
